name: /wtft
tagline: Where The F***ing Tokens?! (WTFT) - Cost Auditing Widget
description: Displays a horizontal, stacked TUI bar chart widget of your session's token costs classified into Spec, Code, and Other work types.

why.scenario: You just finished a coding session and want to see where your token costs went — was it all spec-writing or code refactoring?
why.result: A stacked bar chart widget appears showing token costs broken into Spec, Code, and Other categories over 1-hour intervals. You see exactly which hours burned the most tokens.

why.scenario: You're tracking costs over a full workday and want finer granularity.
why.result: The last 48 four-hour intervals are displayed, giving you a two-day cost breakdown at quarter-day resolution.

why.scenario: You're auditing Claude Code sessions (not Pi) to compare costs across harnesses.
why.result: The widget pulls session data from your Claude Code session directory instead of Pi's, rendering the same bar chart for Claude interactions.

why.scenario: You want to scroll through a long cost history that doesn't fit in the TUI widget.
why.result: A fullscreen scrollable pager overlay opens with the expanded cost timeline.

why.scenario: You want to see the exact USD amount on your billing invoice or reconcile costs across multiple providers.
why.result: WTFT uses public pricing estimates per model and excludes things like caching discounts, free-tier credits, or provider-specific billing nuances. It's a directional cost-awareness tool — not an accounting ledger.

usage.desc: Group cost data into arbitrary binned intervals of minutes, hours, days, or weeks (e.g., 1m, 7m, 4h, 1d, 2w; default: 1h).
Limit the number of interval bars displayed (default: 10).
Set the maximum character width of the TUI widget (default: 240).
Set the display timezone (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London, Asia/Tokyo) to avoid UTC display on servers (default: system local).
Render bars as cumulative sums over time (each bar is sum of current and all prior intervals) (default behavior).
Render bars as discrete interval cost buckets.
Display proportional cost scale tick marks above the bars (default behavior).
Disable cost scale tick marks above the bars.
Launch an interactive scrollable fullscreen pager overlay to view expanded cost history.
Debug 'other' interactions with a bash command histogram.
Show or refresh the WTFT cost-auditing widget (default behavior).
Disable emoji icons and use clean single-width ASCII characters for TUI widgets.
Enable emoji icons in TUI widgets.
Explain why you'd run this tool, with user scenarios and anti-use-cases.

examples.desc: Analyze session cost breakdown in a widget below the editor (240 chars wide).
Group token costs into custom 7-minute intervals.
Render binned date/time labels in Eastern Standard Time.
Show last 20 4-hour interval bars of cumulative token costs.
Force show or refresh the cost audit widget.
View a full, scrollable cost audit timeline inside a fullscreen TUI pager.
Debug 'other' interactions with a bash command histogram.
Hide the cost audit widget from the display.
